# A Theory of Justice

*[Rawls](../philosophers/rawls.md) · 1971*

A landmark of twentieth-century political philosophy. Rawls seeks the principles of a just society by asking which ones we would choose behind a 'veil of ignorance', not knowing the place we will occupy in it. From this he draws the difference principle: inequalities are just only if they improve the lot of the least advantaged.
